{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks whether belimumab is used for lupus nephritis. Reviewing the provided documents, multiple sources confirm that belimumab is approved for the treatment of lupus nephritis. Document [0] explicitly states that belimumab was approved in 2020 for induction therapy in lupus nephritis in addition to standard MMF/cyclophosphamide/steroid protocols. It also references the BLISS-LN study that demonstrated its effectiveness, leading to approval. Document [1] lists belimumab as one of the two drugs approved for use in lupus nephritis. Document [17] mentions that belimumab can be used until the second trimester in pregnant patients with lupus nephritis, further supporting its use. Although the UMLS documents do not mention belimumab, they focus on other treatments like hydroxychloroquine and corticosteroids. The MEDCORP documents consistently support the use of belimumab in lupus nephritis. Therefore, based on the evidence, belimumab is indeed used for lupus nephritis.", "answer_choice": "A"}
